2605.00506.title: Surprisal Minimisation over Goal-directed Alternatives Predicts Production Choice in Dialogue
2605.00506.paper_summary: We model utterance production as probabilistic cost-sensitive choice over contextual alternatives, using information-theoretic notions of cost. We distinguish between goal-directed alternatives that realise a fixed communicative intent and goal-agnostic alternatives defined only by contextual plausibility, allowing us to derive speaker- and listener-oriented interpretations of different cost measures. We present a procedure to generate both types of alternative sets using language models. Analysing production choices in open-ended dialogue under both deterministic and probabilistic cost minimisation, we find that surprisal minimisation relative to goal-directed alternatives provides the strongest predictive account under both analyses. By contrast, uniform information density and length-based costs exhibit weaker and less consistent predictive power across conditions. More broadly, our study suggests that alternative-conditioned optimisation with LM-generated alternatives provides a principled framework for studying speaker and listener pressures in naturalistic language production.
2605.00506.paper_summary_zh: 我們將話語產出建模為基於上下文替代選項的概率性成本敏感選擇，使用信息論的成本概念。<lb/>我們區分實現固定交際意圖的目標導向替代選項和僅由上下文合理性定義的目標無關替代選項，這使我們能夠推導出不同成本度量的說話者和聽者導向解釋。<lb/>我們提出了一種使用語言模型生成這兩種類型替代集的程序。<lb/>在開放式對話中分析在確定性和概率性成本最小化下的產出選擇，我們發現，相對於目標導向替代選項的驚訝度最小化在兩種分析中提供了最強的預測解釋。<lb/>相比之下，均勻信息密度和基於長度的成本在各種條件下顯示出較弱且不一致的預測能力。<lb/>更廣泛地說，我們的研究表明，基於替代的優化與LM生成的替代選項提供了一個原則性框架，用於研究自然語言產出中的說話者和聽者壓力。
2605.00506.author: Tom Utting et.al.
2605.00506.authors: Tom Utting, Mario Giulianelli, Arabella Sinclair
